dataset: Chinook Database (public sample database from GitHub)

pi_items.source: Task setup script (setup_task.sh) confirms this is the working directory path where the database is restored

pi_items.verified_value: Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite (official filename from lerocha/chinook-database GitHub repository)
pi_items.source: GitHub repository https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database/blob/master/ChinookDatabase/DataSources/Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite confirms the filename is 'Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite'
Matches the task description requirement: 'Save the query as DiverseCustomerReport'

Database query execution on Chinook SQLite database verified 57 customers have exactly 5 or more distinct genres. Maximum genre count is 12 (Luis Rojas), minimum for filter is 5 (Emma Jones, Kara Nielsen, Roberto Almeida, Stanisław Wójcik)
Task description requirement: 'CustomerName': First Name and Last Name concatenated with a space (e.g., 'Latoya Mitchell')
Task description requirement: 'DiversityRating': The count of distinct genres followed by the text ' Genres' (e.g., '6 Genres')

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database is a real, public sample database from GitHub's lerocha/chinook-database repository, created by Luis Rocha using real data from an iTunes library. The database contains 11 tables including Customer, Invoice, InvoiceLine, Track, and Genre tables required for the task. SQL query verification confirms there are 57 customers who have purchased tracks from 5 or more distinct genres (out of 25 total genres). The minimum threshold of 5 genres is verified and achievable. All expected column names and query naming requirements align with the Chinook database schema (FirstName, LastName, GenreId fields exist and are accessible via the required joins).
